identify_root_cause.expected_solution: Agent must identify that markupsafe 2.1.5 removed the deprecated soft_str function (renamed to soft_unicode/soft_str earlier and finally dropped). click.utils (specifically src/click/utils.py) imports and uses markupsafe.soft_str for string coercion in echo() and related helpers, so updating markupsafe in Click's CI environment causes the affected tests (test_echo_stdin_and_stderr, test_echo_color_flag, test_echo_with_encoding, test_open_file_atomic_write) to fail with ImportError or AttributeError on the missing symbol.
evaluation_criteria: Identifies the markupsafe upgrade as the trigger (not Click code regressions)
Names src/click/utils.py as the consumer that imports markupsafe.soft_str
Names the removed/renamed markupsafe.soft_str symbol as the missing API
Lists at least one of the affected tests (e.g. test_echo_stdin_and_stderr)

triage_severity.expected_solution: Severity is medium. The break is contained to Click's CI / test environment when paired with the new markupsafe — end-user Click installations are unaffected unless they also pull markupsafe>=2.1.5 alongside the affected Click version. There is no production data loss or remote exposure; the user impact is limited to developers building/installing Click from a fresh environment that resolves the new markupsafe.
evaluation_criteria: Triages severity as medium (not critical, not low)
Notes that end users running pinned environments are unaffected
Identifies CI / fresh-install developer environments as the impact surface

communication.expected_solution: Agent emits a 'communicate' action that informs Click maintainers and downstream stakeholders (release manager, dependent project owners) of the incident: scope (CI failures only), root cause (markupsafe 2.1.5 removed soft_str), affected tests, planned remediation, and an ETA. The communication should land before deploy/rollback actions and reference a tracking issue or post.
evaluation_criteria: Emits at least one action with action_type 'communicate'
Communication targets stakeholders (maintainers, release manager) rather than only the agent's own log
Communication summarizes root cause, scope, and planned remediation

remediation.expected_solution: Agent emits a 'remediate' action that either (a) pins markupsafe<2.1.5 in Click's setup.cfg/pyproject.toml as a short-term fix, or (b) replaces the markupsafe.soft_str call sites in src/click/utils.py with a compatible alternative (str() coercion or markupsafe.escape, depending on the original intent). A code_patch artifact under /workspace pointing at src/click/utils.py is preferred; a dependency pin is acceptable as a tactical fix.
evaluation_criteria: Emits at least one action with action_type 'remediate'
Remediation either pins markupsafe<2.1.5 or modifies src/click/utils.py to drop the soft_str dependency
Remediation targets the correct file (src/click/utils.py) or the correct dependency manifest
